mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in Industrial Production (INDPRO) indicate expanding manufacturing output, leading to higher corporate earnings and upward revisions, which propel S&P 500 prices higher over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation between monthly INDPRO changes and 5-day forward returns is negative or below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) below its 3-month moving average signals improving labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If UNRATE continues to decline but S&P 500 returns are negative over 60-day windows in more than 40% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in Housing Starts (HOUST) and Retail and Food Services Sales (RSAFS) indicate strengthening consumer demand and residential investment, jointly driving S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: both HOUST and RSAFS increase month-over-month
failure_condition: If both proxies rise but SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of such months over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero-interest-rate environment (FEDFUNDS <= 0.25%), further monetary easing is not possible via rate cuts, so any increase in FEDFUNDS signals tightening expectations that drag on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by at least 25bp but SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) indicate rising inflation that erodes real corporate earnings and anticipates tighter monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL increases more than 0.2% month-over-month but SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in Nonfarm Payrolls (PAYEMS) greater than 200,000 signals strong labor market momentum that boosts investor confidence and near-term corporate outlook, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases by more than 200k but SPX 5-day return is negative in more than 40% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in real GDP (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter and a decline in the Unemployment Rate (UNRATE) month-over-month confirms synchronized economic growth that strongly boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 qoq growth > 0 and UNRATE mom decline > 0
failure_condition: If both conditions hold but SPX 60-day return is negative in more than 30% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: During a period of low inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year < 2.5%), rising Industrial Production (INDPRO) combined with falling Retail Sales (RSAFS) signals weak demand relative to supply, indicating a potential inventory buildup that leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO up and RSAFS down month-over-month
regime_hint: low inflation regime with supply outpacing demand
failure_condition: If the condition holds but SPX 20-day return is positive in more than 60% of cases.
